001package guiAdminHome;
002
003import java.util.ArrayList;
004import java.util.List;
005
006import javafx.collections.FXCollections;
007import javafx.geometry.Pos;
008import javafx.scene.Scene;
009import javafx.scene.control.Alert;
010import javafx.scene.control.Button;
011import javafx.scene.control.ComboBox;
012import javafx.scene.control.Label;
013import javafx.scene.control.TextField;
014import javafx.scene.control.Alert.AlertType;
015import javafx.scene.layout.Pane;
016import javafx.scene.shape.Line;
017import javafx.scene.text.Font;
018import javafx.stage.Stage;
019import database.Database;
020import entityClasses.User;
021import guiUserUpdate.ViewUserUpdate;
022
023/*******
024 * <p> Title: GUIAdminHomePage Class. </p>
025 * 
026 * <p> Description: The Java/FX-based Admin Home Page.  This class provides the JavaFX GUI widgets
027 * that enable an admin to perform admin functions.  This page contains a number of buttons that
028 * have not yet been implemented.  What has been implemented may not work the way the final product
029 * requires and there maybe defects in this code.
030 * 
031 * The class has been written using a singleton design pattern and is the View portion of the 
032 * Model, View, Controller pattern.  The pattern is designed that the all accesses to this page and
033 * its functions starts by invoking the static method displayAdminHome.  No other method should 
034 * attempt to instantiate this class as that is controlled by displayAdminHome.  It ensure that
035 * only one instance of class is instantiated and that one is properly configured for each use.  
036 * 
037 * Please note that this implementation is not appropriate for concurrent systems with multiple
038 * users. This Baeldung article provides insight into the issues: 
039 *           https://www.baeldung.com/java-singleton</p>
040 * 
041 * <p> Copyright: Lynn Robert Carter © 2025 </p>
042 * 
043 * @author Lynn Robert Carter
044 * 
045 * @version 1.00                2025-08-17 Initial version
046 *  
047 */
048
049public class ViewAdminHome {
050        
051        /*-*******************************************************************************************
052
053        Attributes
054        
055        */
056        
057        // These are the application values required by the user interface
058        
059        private static double width = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_WIDTH;
060        private static double height = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.WINDOW_HEIGHT;
061
062        
063        // These are the widget attributes for the GUI. There are 5 areas for this GUI.
064        
065        // GUI Area 1: It informs the user about the purpose of this page, whose account is being used,
066        // and a button to allow this user to update the account settings
067        protected static Label label_PageTitle = new Label();
068        protected static Label label_UserDetails = new Label();
069        protected static Button button_UpdateThisUser = new Button("Account Update");
070
071        // This is a separator and it is used to partition the GUI for various tasks
072        private static Line line_Separator1 = new Line(20, 95, width-20, 95);
073
074        // GUI Area 2: This area is used to provide status of the system.  This basic foundational code
075        // does not have much current status information to display.
076        protected static Label label_NumberOfUsers = new Label("Number of Users: x");
077        
078        // This is a separator and it is used to partition the GUI for various tasks
079        private static Line line_Separator2 = new Line(20, 165, width-20, 165);
080        
081        // GUI Area 3: Invitation functionality for admins
082        protected static Button button_InviteUsers = new Button("Invite New Users");
083        
084        // This is a separator and it is used to partition the GUI for various tasks
085        private static Line line_Separator3 = new Line(20, 255, width-20, 255);
086        
087        // GUI Area 4: This is the second of the two action item areas.  This provides a set of other
088        // admin buttons to use to perform other roles.  Many of these buttons are just stubs and an
089        // alert pops up to inform the admin of this fact.
090        protected static Button button_SetOnetimePassword = new Button("Set a One-Time Password");
091        protected static Button button_DiscussionBoard = new Button("Discussion Board");
092        protected static Button button_DeleteUser = new Button("Delete a User");
093        protected static Button button_ListUsers = new Button("List All Users");
094        protected static Button button_AddRemoveRoles = new Button("Add/Remove Roles");
095        protected static Button button_TeacherGradebook = new Button("Teacher Gradebook");
096        protected static Alert alertNotImplemented = new Alert(AlertType.INFORMATION);
097
098        // This is a separator and it is used to partition the GUI for various tasks
099        private static Line line_Separator4 = new Line(20, 525, width-20,525);
100
101        // GUI Area 5: This is last of the GUI areas.  It is used for quitting the application, logging
102        // out, and on other pages a return is provided so the user can return to a previous page when
103        // the actions on that page are complete.  Be advised that in most cases in this code, the 
104        // return is to a fixed page as opposed to the actual page that invoked the pages.
105        protected static Button button_BackToLogin = new Button("Back to Login");
106        protected static Button button_Logout = new Button("Logout");
107        protected static Button button_Quit = new Button("Quit");
108
109        // This is the end of the GUI objects for the page.
110        
111        // These attributes are used to configure the page and populate it with this user's information
112        private static ViewAdminHome theView;           // Used to determine if instantiation of the class
113                                                                                                // is needed
114
115        // Reference for the in-memory database so this package has access
116        private static Database theDatabase = applicationMain.FoundationsMain.database;
117        
118        protected static Stage theStage;                        // The Stage that JavaFX has established for us
119        private static Pane theRootPane;                        // The Pane that holds all the GUI widgets 
120        protected static User theUser;                          // The current logged in User
121
122        private static Scene theAdminHomeScene;         // The shared Scene each invocation populates
123        private static final int theRole = 1;           // Admin: 1; Role1: 2; Role2: 3
124
125        /*-*******************************************************************************************
126
127        Constructors
128        
129        */
130
131        /**********
132         * <p> Method: displayAdminHome(Stage ps, User user) </p>
133         * 
134         * <p> Description: This method is the single entry point from outside this package to cause
135         * the Admin Home page to be displayed.
136         * 
137         * It first sets up every shared attributes so we don't have to pass parameters.
138         * 
139         * It then checks to see if the page has been setup.  If not, it instantiates the class, 
140         * initializes all the static aspects of the GIUI widgets (e.g., location on the page, font,
141         * size, and any methods to be performed).
142         * 
143         * After the instantiation, the code then populates the elements that change based on the user
144         * and the system's current state.  It then sets the Scene onto the stage, and makes it visible
145         * to the user.
146         * 
147         * @param ps specifies the JavaFX Stage to be used for this GUI and it's methods
148         * 
149         * @param user specifies the User for this GUI and it's methods
150         * 
151         */
152        public static void displayAdminHome(Stage ps, User user) {
153                
154                // Establish the references to the GUI and the current user
155                theStage = ps;
156                theUser = user;
157                
158                // If not yet established, populate the static aspects of the GUI
159                if (theView == null) theView = new ViewAdminHome();             // Instantiate singleton if needed
160                
161                // Populate the dynamic aspects of the GUI with the data from the user and the current
162                // state of the system.
163                theDatabase.getUserAccountDetails(user.getUserName());          // Fetch this user's data
164                applicationMain.FoundationsMain.activeHomePage = theRole;       // Set this as the active Home                                                                                                                                  // UserUpdate page
165
166                                
167                // Set the title for the window, display the page, and wait for the Admin to do something
168                theStage.setTitle("CSE 360 Foundation Code: Admin Home Page");
169                theStage.setScene(theAdminHomeScene);                                           // Set this page onto the stage
170                theStage.show();                                                                                        // Display it to the user
171        }
172        
173        /**********
174         * <p> Method: GUIAdminHomePage() </p>
175         * 
176         * <p> Description: This method initializes all the elements of the graphical user interface.
177         * This method determines the location, size, font, color, and change and event handlers for
178         * each GUI object.
179         * 
180         * This is a singleton and is only performed once.  Subsequent uses fill in the changeable
181         * fields using the displayAdminHome method.</p>
182         * 
183         */
184        private ViewAdminHome() {
185
186                // Create the Pane for the list of widgets and the Scene for the window
187                theRootPane = new Pane();
188                theAdminHomeScene = new Scene(theRootPane, width, height);
189        
190                // Populate the window with the title and other common widgets and set their static state
191                
192                // GUI Area 1
193                label_PageTitle.setText("Admin Home Page");
194                setupLabelUI(label_PageTitle, "Arial", 28, width, Pos.CENTER, 0, 5);
195
196                label_UserDetails.setText("User: " + theUser.getUserName());
197                setupLabelUI(label_UserDetails, "Arial", 20, width, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 20, 55);
198                
199                setupButtonUI(button_UpdateThisUser, "Dialog", 18, 170, Pos.CENTER, 610, 45);
200                button_UpdateThisUser.setOnAction((_) -> 
201                                {ViewUserUpdate.displayUserUpdate(theStage, theUser);});
202                
203                // GUI Area 2
204                setupLabelUI(label_NumberOfUsers, "Arial", 20, 200, Pos.BASELINE_LEFT, 20, 105);
205                label_NumberOfUsers.setText("Number of users: " + 
206                                theDatabase.getNumberOfUsers());
207        
208                // GUI Area 3 - Invitation functionality
209        
210                // GUI Area 4
211                setupButtonUI(button_SetOnetimePassword, "Dialog", 16, 250, Pos.CENTER, 20, 150);
212                button_SetOnetimePassword.setOnAction((_) -> 
213                        {ControllerAdminHome.setOnetimePassword(); });
214
215                setupButtonUI(button_InviteUsers, "Dialog", 16, 250, Pos.CENTER, 280, 150);
216                button_InviteUsers.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerAdminHome.inviteUsers(); });
217
218                setupButtonUI(button_DiscussionBoard, "Dialog", 16, 250, Pos.CENTER, 550, 150);
219                button_DiscussionBoard.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerAdminHome.openDiscussionBoard(); });
220                
221                setupButtonUI(button_DeleteUser, "Dialog", 16, 250, Pos.CENTER, 20, 200);
222                button_DeleteUser.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerAdminHome.deleteUser(); });
223
224                setupButtonUI(button_ListUsers, "Dialog", 16, 250, Pos.CENTER, 20, 250);
225                button_ListUsers.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerAdminHome.listUsers(); });
226
227                setupButtonUI(button_AddRemoveRoles, "Dialog", 16, 250, Pos.CENTER, 20, 300);
228                button_AddRemoveRoles.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerAdminHome.addRemoveRoles(); });
229
230                setupButtonUI(button_TeacherGradebook, "Dialog", 16, 250, Pos.CENTER, 280, 300);
231                button_TeacherGradebook.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerAdminHome.openTeacherGradebook(); });
232                
233                // GUI Area 5
234                setupButtonUI(button_BackToLogin, "Dialog", 18, 250, Pos.CENTER, 580, 540);
235                button_BackToLogin.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerAdminHome.performBackToLogin(); });
236                
237                setupButtonUI(button_Logout, "Dialog", 18, 250, Pos.CENTER, 20, 540);
238                button_Logout.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerAdminHome.performLogout(); });
239    
240                setupButtonUI(button_Quit, "Dialog", 18, 250, Pos.CENTER, 300, 540);
241                button_Quit.setOnAction((_) -> {ControllerAdminHome.performQuit(); });
242
243                // This is the end of the GUI initialization code
244                
245                // Place all of the widget items into the Root Pane's list of children
246                theRootPane.getChildren().addAll(
247                        label_PageTitle, label_UserDetails, button_UpdateThisUser, line_Separator1,
248                        label_NumberOfUsers,
249                        line_Separator2,
250                        button_InviteUsers,
251                        button_DiscussionBoard,
252                        line_Separator3,
253                        button_SetOnetimePassword,
254                        button_DeleteUser,
255                button_ListUsers,
256                button_AddRemoveRoles,
257                        button_TeacherGradebook,
258                line_Separator4, 
259                button_BackToLogin,
260                button_Logout,
261                button_Quit
262                );
263                
264                // With theRootPane set up with the common widgets, it is up to displayAdminHome to show
265                // that Pane to the user after the dynamic elements of the widgets have been updated.
266        }
267
268        /*-*******************************************************************************************
269
270        Helper methods used to minimizes the number of lines of code needed above
271        
272        */
273
274        /**********
275         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a label
276         * 
277         * @param l             The Label object to be initialized
278         * @param ff    The font to be used
279         * @param f             The size of the font to be used
280         * @param w             The width of the Button
281         * @param p             The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
282         * @param x             The location from the left edge (x axis)
283         * @param y             The location from the top (y axis)
284         */
285        private void setupLabelUI(Label l, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y){
286                l.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
287                l.setMinWidth(w);
288                l.setAlignment(p);
289                l.setLayoutX(x);
290                l.setLayoutY(y);                
291        }
292        
293        
294        /**********
295         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a button
296         * 
297         * @param b             The Button object to be initialized
298         * @param ff    The font to be used
299         * @param f             The size of the font to be used
300         * @param w             The width of the Button
301         * @param p             The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
302         * @param x             The location from the left edge (x axis)
303         * @param y             The location from the top (y axis)
304         */
305        private void setupButtonUI(Button b, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y){
306                b.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
307                b.setMinWidth(w);
308                b.setAlignment(p);
309                b.setLayoutX(x);
310                b.setLayoutY(y);                
311        }
312
313        
314        /**********
315         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a text input field
316         * 
317         * @param b             The TextField object to be initialized
318         * @param ff    The font to be used
319         * @param f             The size of the font to be used
320         * @param w             The width of the Button
321         * @param p             The alignment (e.g. left, centered, or right)
322         * @param x             The location from the left edge (x axis)
323         * @param y             The location from the top (y axis)
324         * @param e             Is this TextField user editable?
325         */
326        private void setupTextUI(TextField t, String ff, double f, double w, Pos p, double x, double y, boolean e){
327                t.setFont(Font.font(ff, f));
328                t.setMinWidth(w);
329                t.setMaxWidth(w);
330                t.setAlignment(p);
331                t.setLayoutX(x);
332                t.setLayoutY(y);                
333                t.setEditable(e);
334        }       
335
336        
337        /**********
338         * Private local method to initialize the standard fields for a ComboBox
339         * 
340         * @param c             The ComboBox object to be initialized
341         * @param ff    The font to be used
342         * @param f             The size of the font to be used
343         * @param w             The width of the ComboBox
344         * @param x             The location from the left edge (x axis)
345         * @param y             The location from the top (y axis)
346         */
347        private void setupComboBoxUI(ComboBox <String> c, String ff, double f, double w, double x, double y){
348                c.setStyle("-fx-font: " + f + " " + ff + ";");
349                c.setMinWidth(w);
350                c.setLayoutX(x);
351                c.setLayoutY(y);
352        }
353}
